evals.prompt: I just added a products module to this shop-lib package. Can you make sure my changes match the rest of the codebase's style before I commit?
evals.expected_output: The off-style uncommitted changes (src/products.js + the appended line in src/index.js) are aligned to the project's conventions: 2-space indent, single quotes, semicolons, const/let (no var), descriptive callback params, camelCase locals. Pre-existing files are untouched and behavior is unchanged.
expectations: src/products.js uses 2-space indentation, not 4-space (matches the rest of src/)
src/products.js uses single quotes, not double quotes
Statements in src/products.js end with semicolons
src/products.js uses const/let and contains no `var`
Callback parameters in src/products.js are descriptive words, not single characters (no `.map(p =>` or `.reduce((a, b) =>`)
Local variables in src/products.js are camelCase, not snake_case (no total_price / with_tax)
The appended re-export line in src/index.js was aligned to single quotes + semicolon
The pre-existing files src/users.js, src/orders.js, src/utils.js were NOT modified
Behavior is unchanged: the exported function names and their computed logic are the same, only style/formatting/local-naming changed

evals.prompt: I wrote a new pricing.py for our store package. Please make it consistent with how the rest of the code is written.
evals.expected_output: store/pricing.py is aligned to the project's Python conventions learned from store/users.py and store/orders.py: 4-space indent, double quotes, snake_case, type hints on signatures, f-strings, and docstrings. Behavior unchanged; baseline modules untouched.
expectations: store/pricing.py uses 4-space indentation, not 2-space (matches the rest of the package)
Function and variable names in store/pricing.py are snake_case, not camelCase (no calcTotal / lineItems / withTax)
store/pricing.py uses double quotes, not single quotes (matches the project)
Function signatures in store/pricing.py have type hints, like store/users.py and store/orders.py
String formatting in store/pricing.py uses f-strings, not %-formatting or .format()
store/pricing.py functions have docstrings in the project's style (the neighbor modules all have them)
The pre-existing files store/users.py and store/orders.py were NOT modified
Behavior is unchanged: the functions still compute the same total/summary, only naming/formatting/quotes/types/docstrings were adjusted

evals.prompt: I'm on my feature/add-widget branch and about to open a PR. Go over what I changed and make the style match the rest of the app.
evals.expected_output: Despite a clean working tree, the skill detects no uncommitted changes and compares the branch against main, finds the committed src/widget.js, and aligns it to the baseline files' style (2-space indent, single quotes, semicolons, const/let, descriptive params, camelCase). Baseline files untouched; behavior unchanged.
expectations: The skill correctly determined there were no uncommitted changes and compared the branch against the default branch (main) to find what changed
src/widget.js now uses 2-space indentation, single quotes, and semicolons (matches src/cart.js and src/format.js)
src/widget.js uses const/let and contains no `var`
Single-character callback parameters in src/widget.js were renamed to descriptive names (no `.map(i =>` or `.filter(x =>`)
Local variables in src/widget.js are camelCase, not snake_case (no visible_items)
The pre-existing files src/cart.js and src/format.js were NOT modified
Behavior is unchanged: buildWidget still returns the same { ids, count } shape with the same values

evals.prompt: I added a geometry helper to this package. Can you make it match how the rest of the code is written before I push?
evals.expected_output: src/geometry.js is converted to the project's unusual 'house style' learned from src/math.js and src/strings.js: `function` declarations (not arrow-const) with a space before the parens, no semicolons, 2-space indent, and descriptive parameter names instead of single-char a/b. Pre-existing files untouched; behavior unchanged. This eval checks the core principle of matching the project's idioms even when they differ from mainstream defaults.
expectations: src/geometry.js uses `function` declarations for its exports (export function distance ...), not arrow-const, matching the house style
src/geometry.js puts a space before the function parens (e.g. `function distance (`), matching the neighbor files
src/geometry.js has NO semicolons, matching the StandardJS house style used by the neighbors
src/geometry.js uses 2-space indentation, not 4-space
The single-character parameters `a`/`b` were renamed to descriptive names
The pre-existing files src/math.js and src/strings.js were NOT modified
Behavior is unchanged: distance returns sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) and midpoint returns the averaged point

evals.prompt: I just added a variance function to analytics/aggregate.py. Please make my change consistent with the rest of the codebase.
evals.expected_output: Only the newly added function in analytics/aggregate.py is aligned to the clean house style (snake_case, 4-space, type hints, docstring, descriptive locals). The pre-existing committed legacy function in the same file is left untouched, and no other file changes. This eval checks the surgical / no-over-reach guarantee.
expectations: The newly added function was renamed to snake_case (calc_variance), not left camelCase
The newly added function uses 4-space indentation, matching the clean code in the package
The newly added function has a type-hinted signature and a docstring, like mean() and describe()
Single-character locals in the new function (m, x) were renamed to descriptive names
The pre-existing committed legacy function `legacyMean` was left UNCHANGED (still camelCase, still 2-space indented, still uses `for v in values`)
The pre-existing file analytics/summary.py was NOT modified
Behavior is unchanged: the new function still computes population variance (mean of squared deviations from the mean)
